name: Shaun King
subject: Stacey Abrams and Andrew Gillum need us right now
body:
Right now votes are still being counted in Florida and Georgia and the vote margins are heading in our favor.
#
Friends,
Right now votes are still being counted in Florida and Georgia and the vote margins are heading in our favor. Andrew Gillum and Stacey Abrams are committed to fighting to make sure every vote is counted. But they need our help.
**[Can you split a contribution to support Stacey Abrams' and Andrew Gillum's campaigns to ensure they have the staff and resources to contact provisional voters, fight the legal battles, and make sure every voice is heard? They need your help to counter the election-rigging in these Republican-controlled states.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a719/bed43eb/8b4c5342/75fc5179/1070777348/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/count-every-vote-ourrev?refcode=em181114-full&noskip=true")**
This year saw outrageous voter suppression by Republicans in states like Georgia and Florida that have historically disenfranchised minority populations. They will do everything they can to prevent these votes from being counted.
Georgia is on the verge of a runoff election if the Republican candidate's vote share drops below 50% - he is at 50.3% right now with mail and provisional ballots still in play. The margin in Florida is just 0.4%, within the threshold of a state-mandated recount with overseas and military ballots yet to be counted.
**[Can you give $5 to Stacey Abrams' and Andrew Gillum's campaigns right now so they have the legal staffing to ensure accountability in the vote counting processes in their states? This is so important.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a719/bed43eb/8b4c5342/75fc5179/1070777348/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/count-every-vote-ourrev?refcode=em181114-full&noskip=true")**
Fairness and accountability in our elections should not be a revolutionary idea, but it seems that in today's America we cannot take anything for granted. We must continue to strive for a country where each person can vote. Thank you for doing your part to move us closer to that reality.
In solidarity,
Shaun King
Writer and Activist

subject: President Trump makes announcement on prison reform
body:
Shortly after 4:30 p.m. today, President Donald J. Trump called on Congress to take action and support the FIRST STEP Act...

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0129d3e9b9&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • November 14, 2018

** President Trump backs historic prison reform
------------------------------------------------------------
Shortly after 4:30 p.m. today, President Donald J. Trump called on Congress to take action and support the FIRST STEP Act, a bipartisan bill that will reform America’s prisons and improve our Nation’s criminal justice system.

“Our whole Nation benefits if former inmates are able to reenter society as productive, law-abiding citizens,” the President says.

One-in-three American adults today has some type of criminal record, and more than 2 million Americans are in prison—including 181,000 in Federal penitentiaries. More than 95 percent of these inmates will eventually leave prison and face the challenge of restarting their lives. Among Federal inmates today, nearly 40 percent will be rearrested within five years of release.

That outcome helps no one. The FIRST STEP Act will make our communities safer by making our justice system work better in three key ways:
1. Provide incentives for low-risk inmates to receive crucial support services, including vocational training and faith-based programs to ease reentry

2. House more prisoners in facilities closer to their own communities, allowing for family visitation and greater local support

3. Roll back certain provisions of former President Clinton’s infamous crime law, reforming mandatory minimums that have led to racially discriminatory outcomes and increased prison overcrowding

Get the facts: President Trump backs historic prison reform (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0e706107f2&e=3ee4985eea)

Watch: President Trump’s announcement from the White House (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bc1c7e7052&e=3ee4985eea)

** Vice President Pence’s trip to Asia
------------------------------------------------------------
Vice President Mike Pence is in Asia this week, representing the United States in a series of key summits throughout the region. His mission is simple: advance President Trump’s vision of a free and open Indo-Pacific.

“The United States seeks collaboration, not control,” the Vice President wrote in The Washington Post (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5840851b28&e=3ee4985eea) before departing. “We seek an Indo-Pacific — from the United States to India, from Japan to Australia, and everywhere in between — where sovereignty is respected, where commerce flows unhindered and where independent nations are masters of their own destinies.”

On North Korea, the Vice President was clear. “The pressure campaign will continue, and the sanctions will remain in full force, until we achieve the final, fully verified denuclearization,” he said in a joint press statement alongside Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe. “The United States, Japan, and the world will accept nothing less.”

1 minute highlight: Vice President Pence’s joint statement with Prime Minister Abe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1582563dd6&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian
President Donald J. Trump speaks on the phone with FEMA Administrator Brock Long to receive the latest update on the devastating wildfires in California | November 14, 2018

Privacy Policy (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=331346219b&e=3ee4985eea) | Contact the White House (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=797b28c97a&e=3ee4985eea) | Unsubscribe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=1a8234d5d5)

name: Stacey Abrams
subject: This election has tested our faith.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor
body:
This election has tested our faith. Our faith in our leaders and in our election system. |
---
[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa619/34a064cc/5ad4192/70c0c9a/2901230258/VEsH/ "https://staceyabrams.com/")

| | | I will be honest: This election has tested our faith. Our faith in our leaders and in our election system. But I'm asking you not to give up, to keep your faith in our democracy. And democracy demands that we do our best for everyone, every day. That is why my team is still working hard to make sure that the system works for everyone. No matter where you come from, who you voted for or how you voted, if you put your trust in our system, we will not stop fighting until those votes are counted. I want to be clear: Voter suppression is not new. My parents had to fight for their right to vote in the Jim Crow South. As a teenager, my dad was arrested for helping Black folks register to vote. My mother was involved in the civil rights movement, too. Now, their daughter is running for Governor. This journey wasn't easy. Generations of hard work - most of which went unrecognized - have culminated in this moment, right now. And we're not done yet. I will continue the fight that generations of Georgians have fought before me. I will not stop fighting until we have guaranteed not only that every vote is counted in this election, but that every vote will be counted in the next election, and the one after that. Can I count on you to see this through with me? **[Make a $3 donation to support our team as we work to ensure every ballot cast is a ballot counted, not just today, but tomorrow and every day after that.](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa619/34a064cc/5ad4192/70c0c9b/2901230258/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTE0MjAxOC4yLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11142018.2-full")** This is about the future of our democracy. For Georgia, Stacey Abrams

So What Are We Likely to See in The New Congress? No Images? ### [So What Are We Likely to See in The New Congress?](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-i/)
Well I'm back in Washington, after a month or so being home in Cincinnati, preparing for, and recovering from, the recent election. Again, thank you for having the confidence in me to represent your best interests in the swamp that is Washington nowadays. I'm girding up for battle in what will most certainly be a very different environment - at least in the House. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-d/) _Previous Weekly Posts_ [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-h/)
### [T](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-k/)[hank You So Very Much!](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-u/)
First, thank you so very much for your support in this race. I'm so grateful for the opportunity to continue to represent you in Washington.

It was a late night last night watching all the election results coming in from around the state and across the nation. So I'll keep this week's blog short. Click the link below to read my thoughts on the night's results. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-o/) [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-b/)
### [A Ceasefire, Quickly Broken](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-n/)
I just got home from a rally at a place in downtown Cincinnati on 6th Street, called The Backstage Event Center. Among the Republican candidates who spoke at this Boisterous event, one week before a major election, were Congressman Warren Davis, Senator Rob Portman, Ohio Attorney General Mike DeWine, and oh yeah, me. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-p/) [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-x/)
### [Two Weeks Out - So How's the Election Looking?](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlhklid-kujkulhluu-m/)
A lot better than it looked two months ago, or two weeks ago for that matter. I think things are trending in the right direction. The Democrats' advantage on the generic ballot has narrowed significantly - from double digits to low single digits. And in the so-called battleground congressional districts, the ones that are hotly contested this time around (like mine), and will decide which party controls the House after the election, the generic ballot is essentially a dead heat. name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: This isn’t a joke
body:
Peter,
The elections should be over by now, but well, they're not.
We’re still waiting on the Senate and Governor’s race in Florida, and the Governor’s race in Georgia.
What a mess.
**I wrote a few days ago that we wouldn’t know the final results of close elections until all of the “forgotten” ballot boxes were pulled out of car trunks, and I was only half joking.**
It should be obvious by now that in really close races, the desperation to win can bring out the ugly side in people. Political gamesmanship kicks in, while laws and decorum are kicked to the side.
In Broward County, Florida, the recount is taking center stage nationally. Under the authority of Brenda Snipes, the elections supervisor who has twice been found guilty of violating election law in the past two years, the number of ballots reported keeps changing. There are anecdotal reports of boxes of votes being left unattended for days, and valid provisional ballots mixed with rejected ballots.
Not surprisingly, Democratic gubernatorial candidate Andrew Gillum has taken back his concession speech as the vote count inexplicably drags on.
In Georgia, the gubernatorial candidate must win 50% of the vote plus one to win outright. Brian Kemp (R) has earned 50.2 percent and Stacey Abrams 48.7 percent, yet Abrams still has not conceded and the race isn’t settled yet. On Sunday, her campaign filed a lawsuit asking a federal judge to order election officials to count rejected ballots and move the election certification deadline to Wednesday.
In the United States, each vote counts because each citizen matters. Yet, when the legitimacy of an election is questioned, the foundation of our country weakens.
There were no extraordinary circumstances in these elections. So, we are dealing with either incompetence or corruption in the counting process. Either way, the rules are set up for a reason. And some want those rules to bend.
Isn’t that true in other issues, too? The rules don’t fit, so they want to bend them.
**Here’s the bottom line.**
You and I must remain vigilant.
In America, there is a tendency to cast your vote and then sit back and think your responsibility is fulfilled until the next election rolls around two years from now. And nothing could be further from the truth.
We must stay involved and informed.
Whether it’s staying involved to protect the integrity of every vote until election outcomes are decided or staying involved to advocate for biblical principles in key policies throughout the new year - our involvement is needed - YOUR involvement is needed.
Some people might tune out the noise of politics, but at My Faith Votes, we are passionate about helping you stay on top of the most current issues affecting your faith. We want you to see where the rules are bending – and where the foundation of our culture is weakening – and help you take **_action_**.
Your faith doesn’t call you to action just once in a while, but all the time. We are committed to keeping you informed and guiding you with specific ways to Pray, Think and Act on the issues of the day.
Our devotionals, small group studies, weekly news summaries, and live interviews with influential Christian leaders are just a few ways we are equipping the body of Christ to take a stand. We must hold our elected officials accountable. Even if you didn’t vote for them, you have a responsibility to pray for them and let them know where you stand.
So, I want to invite you to continue standing with us through your prayers for these undecided elections and through your financial support.
**[Donate today to help Christians take a stand and hold elected officials accountable in the upcoming year](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p181115apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181115apl01nd&n=1).**
We will continue to encourage Christians to be involved at all the times. We stand united to see biblical values advanced and election integrity upheld in every county, every state, and every election because it is the basis of our democratic system.
[](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p181115apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181115apl01nd&n=2)
**United. We Stand.**

Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181115apl01nd&n=3)
©2018 My Faith Votes
10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](dpwillis67@gmail.com).
To stop receiving emails, [click here](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/unsubscribe?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181115apl01nd&n=4).

name: Jane O'Meara Sanders
subject: Foreign policy is more than just war and peace
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
I sat down with Representative Tulsi Gabbard to talk about foreign policy
#
Friend,
While we tend to discuss domestic and foreign policy separately, in truth, they are deeply intertwined. The choices that we make as a country determine our national priorities and reflect our values.
Foreign policy is more than just war and peace, it is a nuanced and complex issue that directly affects us here at home.
I sat down with Representative Tulsi Gabbard to talk about foreign policy, starting with her personal experiences serving our country both at home and abroad, through the government's budget decisions between foreign and domestic spending, and our country's approaches to some of the most contentious current foreign issues.
**[Click here or on the image below to watch our conversation.](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a706/bed43eb/8bef60da/75fc51a3/361394353/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/foreign-policy-is-more-than-just-war-and-peace")**

name: StaceyAbrams.com
subject: Dawn, Danny, and Nedghie.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor
body:
Voter suppression has many different faces. |
---
[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa61c/34a064cc/5b4e066/70c0a3f/3651347707/VEsH/ "https://staceyabrams.com/")

| | | Friend: Voter suppression has many different faces. It can look different depending on who you are and where you live. Over the past week and a half, thousands of Georgians have reached out to our team to share their varied personal experiences with voter suppression in this past election. We wanted to share a few of their stories with you today: [](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa61c/34a064cc/5b4e066/70c0a3c/3651347707/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTE1MjAxOC1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11152018-full") _"I am 89 years old and nearly blind. I had to fill out two mail-in ballot applications because the first was rejected. When I finally received my ballot, filled it out and mailed it back, I was told that my ballot was also rejected. I didn't give up. I applied for a ballot again, and received it on the Saturday before the election, so a family member physically had to deliver it to get it in on time."
\- Dawn Elizabeth Crawford, Gwinnett County _ [](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa61c/34a064cc/5b4e066/70c0a3c/3651347707/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTE1MjAxOC1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11152018-full") _"I voted at the Ponce De Leon Library for the past thirteen years, and then was redistricted and voted for the first time at the Butler Street Baptist Church. The problem was that there were only two people checking voters in, which created a huge bottleneck and drastically slowed things down. There were 15-20 largely vacant electronic voting machines because people were not being checked-in in a timely manner. I waited nearly two hours to vote, and saw people leaving because the lines were too long."
\- Danny Greeson, Atlanta _ [](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa61c/34a064cc/5b4e066/70c0a3c/3651347707/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTE1MjAxOC1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11152018-full") _"I live in DeKalb County, but I am currently attending graduate school in Boston, Massachusetts, so I requested an absentee ballot for the General Election. Despite requesting my absentee ballot weeks in advance and following up with DeKalb's Board of Election's office, I never received my absentee ballot. Like thousands of Georgians, I was denied my constitutional right to vote."
\- Nedghie Adrien_ All of these stories are different. None of them should have happened. Election integrity matters, and we will not stop fighting until every vote is counted. That's why we have to ask: **[Chip in $3 to support our team as we fight to ensure that every vote is counted in this election and that everyone's right to vote is protected.](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa61c/34a064cc/5b4e066/70c0a3c/3651347707/VEsD/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTE1MjAxOC1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11152018-full")** Our team is not just fighting for Stacey Abrams. We are fighting for free and fair elections, we are fighting for Dawn, and Danny, and Nedghie, and _you_. Thank you for taking a moment to read these stories and witness history. Sincerely, \- Team Abrams
---

**[DONATE](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa61c/34a064cc/5b4e066/70c0a3c/3651347707/VEsA/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTE1MjAxOC1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11152018-full")**

subject: Bipartisan criminal justice reform, the migrant caravan, and American energy dominance
body:
USA Today reports that President Donald J. Trump put his full support behind criminal justice reform in an announcement from the White House... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=390079e391&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Embraces Bipartisan Criminal Justice Reform Legislation**

_USA Today_ reports that President Donald J. Trump put his full support behind criminal justice reform in an announcement from the White House yesterday.

“The ‘First Step Act,’ developed by [Jared] Kushner and a bipartisan group of lawmakers, is designed to improve rehabilitation programs for former prisoners and give judges more discretion in sentencing offenders for nonviolent crime, particularly drug offenders,” David Jackson, Deborah Barfield Berry, and John Fritze report.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b9e47f70fa&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| The _[Investor’s Business Daily](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a3f4543709&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes that former President Obama was “determined to force the country to dump billions of taxpayer subsidies on ‘renewable’ energy, and needed a reason to justify it. Yet all along, the truth was that the U.S. could be a global energy powerhouse. Now, with President Donald Trump in the White House calling for U.S. energy dominance, everyone knows that to be the case.”
---
|
---
| “The booming U.S. economy, jump-started by the 2017 GOP tax cut, has created 878,000 more millionaires in just the last 12 months, according to an international wealth report from Credit Suisse,” Paul Bedard reports for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=53fbfbbac0&e=3ee4985eea)_.
“’The boom goes on,’ cheers the Credit Suisse's Global Wealth Report.”
---
|
---
| President Trump’s announcement yesterday on prison reform shows he is ready and willing to work with Democrats on a bipartisan basis, Kayleigh McEnany reports for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=17e1953553&e=3ee4985eea). “White House Senior Advisers Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump are among top Trump administration officials who have laid the groundwork for bipartisanship in an era of divided government – a rare feat indeed.”
---
|

Friend -- It has been one week since you re-elected me to represent Arkansas's Fourth District in Congress. name: Jay Inslee
subject: Count every vote
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Every vote in Florida and Georgia deserves to be counted.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b0cd/2f5ea88d/7b57125a/2cbdb9bb/1545976269/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/count-every-vote?utm_source=ema_2018-11-14_fl-ga-count&source=ema_2018-11-14_fl-ga-count&utm_campaign=fl-ga-count&utm_medium=ema") Friend, There's no way around it: Republicans are trying to suppress our votes. It shouldn't be unreasonable for Stacey Abrams and Andrew Gillum to demand that every vote be counted. But that's exactly what Republicans are trying to prevent. **What we're seeing in Georgia and Florida must be called out for what it is: voter suppression. Every American has a dog in this fight -- our very democracy is at risk.** [**It's time to stand with Andrew Gillum and Stacey Abrams, and stand up for our basic rights. Will you add your name next to mine to demand that every vote be counted?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b0cd/2f5ea88d/7b57125a/2cbdb9bb/1545976269/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/count-every-vote?utm_source=ema_2018-11-14_fl-ga-count&source=ema_2018-11-14_fl-ga-count&utm_campaign=fl-ga-count&utm_medium=ema") | [SIGN THE PETITION »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b0cd/2f5ea88d/7b57125a/2cbdb9bb/1545976269/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/count-every-vote?utm_source=ema_2018-11-14_fl-ga-count&source=ema_2018-11-14_fl-ga-count&utm_campaign=fl-ga-count&utm_medium=ema")
---
From the start of her campaign, Stacey Abrams has been committed to lifting up the voices of Georgians who have been unseen and unheard by our political leaders. And now, after an election marred by irregularities and voter suppression, Brian Kemp has attempted to crown himself governor before all the votes are counted. Meanwhile, the Secretary of State's office is lying about the number of votes cast as well as the number of votes still to be counted. That's just wrong, and we can't stand for this.
In Florida, Andrew Gillum ran a historic campaign that lifted up the voices of huge numbers of Floridians. His election shows that there is power in voting. But Republicans across the country are trying to take that right away: President Trump is making up irresponsible conspiracy theories, while Rick Scott is trying to use law enforcement to stop ballots from being counted. In response, Gillum's team is using every means necessary to ensure that ALL the votes in the state are counted.
**The Gillum and Abrams campaigns are fighting with everything they've got to make sure that every eligible vote cast is a vote counted.** Their teams have swelled with hundreds of dedicated volunteers, staff, and attorneys to ensure every vote is counted. But we need everyone to act, now.
The stakes are so high here. We're looking at two historic wins for African-American candidates in the South and two new Democratic governors. Looking past this election, though, this is about protecting one of our most sacred freedoms we have as Americans. We need to keep up the pressure to make sure these states count every vote -- for this election, and for our future as a democracy.
[**Will you sign our petition to make sure we count every vote?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b0cd/2f5ea88d/7b57125a/2cbdb9bb/1545976269/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/count-every-vote?utm_source=ema_2018-11-14_fl-ga-count&source=ema_2018-11-14_fl-ga-count&utm_campaign=fl-ga-count&utm_medium=ema")
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee
[SIGN THE PETITION »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b0cd/2f5ea88d/7b57125a/2cbdb9bb/1545976269/VEsD/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/count-every-vote?utm_source=ema_2018-11-14_fl-ga-count&source=ema_2018-11-14_fl-ga-count&utm_campaign=fl-ga-count&utm_medium=ema")
---

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Mike Espy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
We could still win another Senate seat this year. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
| | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Derek:

On Election Day -- and in the days since -- we focused a lot on some great wins and some tough losses. But in Mississippi, there was no final answer -- Democrat Mike Espy tied his opponent, the Republican incumbent.

**This is a big deal. We have a real shot at picking up a Senate seat in Mississippi in a November 27th runoff. But that can't happen if Democrats like us sit this race out.** [**Mike needs our help.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f175d/3e0906/7ad10937/3e3740ce/2129508360/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-espy?amount=25&refcode=20181115_1_FR_TK_Espy&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+Mike+needs+our+help&utm_campaign=20181115_1_FR_TK_Espy&source=20181115_1_FR_TK_Espy")

In less than two weeks, Mississippians will head to the polls again to break the tie. Mike is a former Congressman and U.S. Secretary of Agriculture who has fought for job creation in Mississippi and to defend people's health care. subject: The courts are doing what Brian Kemp refused to do.

body:
A judge ruled that all counties must accept absentee ballots with minor discrepancies. |

[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa611/34a064cc/5b3dfe1/70c09fa/1083945578/VEsH/ "https://staceyabrams.com/")

| | | Big news: In an extraordinary victory for voting rights, a judge ruled that all counties must accept absentee ballots with minor discrepancies. That means that tiny errors won't prevent Georgians from having their vote counted. These are ballots which were cast in good faith by thousands of Georgians attempting to exercise their fundamental right to vote, and which were among the countless votes Brian Kemp attempted - but ultimately failed - to suppress in this year's election. Under Kemp's watch, too many Georgians have had substantial roadblocks placed in their path as they sought to exercise their right to vote. Now, the courts are doing what Brian Kemp's Secretary of State office refused to - upholding and protecting Georgia's rights and underlining the need for free and fair elections. This is the fourth Federal judge in the last few days to rule against Brian Kemp and in favor of voting rights. But the battle isn't over yet. We need to keep fighting to ensure that not a single vote is left uncounted. Will you help us? **[Chip in $3 to help us continue our fight to make sure every ballot cast is a ballot counted>>](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa611/34a064cc/5b3dfe1/70c09fb/1083945578/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTE1MjAxOC4yLWMifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11152018.2-c")** This fight is not about our campaign, or about Stacey Abrams.
